title: Kingdom Come: Deliverance

notes: As others have mentioned, post-1.9 update the game's performance has been cut in half. I used to be able to run a mix of settings (max textures, low lighting, everything else somewhere in between) and achieve a locked 60fps even in towns at 1440p, but now regardless of settings or resolution the performance is all over the place, with minimums hitting around 25-30fps. I've played through the final portion of the main storyline, as well as the whole of A Woman's Lot and Band of Bastards on the 1.9 update and didn't encounter any other problems associated with running via Steam Play, and the performance never got so bad that it became unplayable, though it certainly impacted the immersion. Long lines of sight seem to be the thing that mostly drains the framerate for me, for example, I can stand at the upper castle in Rattay and look out over the wall towards Ledechko and I will drop to about 25fps, but turning around on the spot to look in at the castle (the tournament area) full of NPCS and enterable buildings and I will immediately bounce back up to around 50fps, though pre-update this area was easily 70fps+ when stood still, and higher still once I'd left and was looking out at the countryside. Even standing a few feet back from the wall so the view is partially obscured increases performance up to the high 30s, but unfortunately I haven't noticed any improvements from dropping the draw distance sliders down to their minimums. I've tried with and without e-sync, tried Proton 3.7, 3.16 and the latest 4.2 and tried r_BatchType 1 in the console, all with no perceptible improvements. I'm not holding my breath for an update since I haven't seen any WIndows users complaining about performance so it seems unlikely that the developers will change anything since as far as they are concerned there is nothing to fix. Very frustrating!
title: Kingdom Come: Deliverance

https://github.com/GloriousEggroll/proton-ge-custom/releases

title: Kingdom Come: Deliverance

notes:
After playing several hours on the latest 1.9 update with terrible performance, thanks to one of the recent reports here I am glad to say that the my framerate is now far better thanks to using this custom proton build: Proton-4.11-GE-1: https://github.com/GloriousEggroll/proton-ge-custom/releases (the github page provides clear installation and usage instructions, very easy to set up!)

I'm now using this along with the neccesary moved dlls and along with having esync disabled. 1.9 still seems to be running a touch slower than pre-1.9, but not by much. The regular and sustained drops to 25-30 fps encountered while using the standard proton versions are completely gone. I'm still having to play at mostly medium / low to maintain 60fps at 1440p, compared to the high / very high I could run the game at on Windows, but I can at least maintain that 60fps once again, and it is otherwise very playable and enjoyable with no other proton-related problems.

title: Kingdom Come: Deliverance

notes:
Step 1 – Install Winetricks via package manager if not already installed.  

Step 2 – Navigate to Home / .steam / steam / steamapps / common / KindomComeDeliverance / Bin 

Copy the contents from Win64Shared folder to Win64 folder. 

Step 3 – Fix right turning issue with multiple monitors. 

Navigate to Home / .steam / steam / steamapps / compatdata / 379430 

Open up a terminal and type the below: (replace ryan with your username). 

WINEARCH=win64 WINEPREFIX='/home/ryan/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/379430/pfx'
winecfg

Install Gecko twice if prompted. 

In the Wine configuration, choose the Graphics tab and change the following. 

Tick Automatically capture the mouse in full-screen windows and Emulate a virtual desktop

Set Desktop size as 1920 x 1080 (or your resolution).

You should now be all set up. 

Optional – If you have poor performance – Enable GameMode 

See https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Nf3I6hn_Rm8 for a visual tutorial.
